sourceText: 19th-20th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19-20

sourceText: Ralph Waldo Emerson
translatedText: Ralph Waldo Emerson

sourceText: Deep spiritual doubts during his time as a minister, the profound grief from the death of his first wife Ellen Tucker and his young son Waldo, and personal health struggles.
translatedText: Mashaka makubwa ya kiroho wakati wake kama mhudumu, huzuni kubwa kutokana na kifo cha mke wake wa kwanza Ellen Tucker na mwanawe mdogo Waldo, na matatizo ya afya binafsi.

sourceText: He found solace and renewed purpose through philosophical inquiry, embracing his role as a public intellectual and writer, and articulating his vision of self-reliance and spiritual intuition.
translatedText: Alipata kitulizo na kusudi jipya kupitia uchunguzi wa kifalsafa, akikumbatia jukumu lake kama msomi na mwandishi wa umma, na kueleza maono yake ya kujitegemea na angavu ya kiroho.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Susan B Anthony
translatedText: Susan B Anthony

sourceText: She experienced profound pain from the societal injustice of women being denied the right to vote and treated as second-class citizens. The movement itself brought constant opposition, public ridicule, and even threats of arrest and imprisonment.
translatedText: Alipata maumivu makali kutokana na dhuluma ya kijamii ya wanawake kunyimwa haki ya kupiga kura na kutibiwa kama raia wa daraja la pili. Harakati zenyewe zilileta upinzani wa mara kwa mara, dhihaka hadharani, na hata vitisho vya kukamatwa na kufungwa.

sourceText: Anthony tirelessly lectured, organized, and lobbied for legislative change, refusing to be deterred. She found strength in solidarity with fellow activists, maintaining hope and perseverance through immense challenges.
translatedText: Anthony bila kuchoka alitoa mhadhara, kupanga, na kushawishi mabadiliko ya sheria, akikataa kuzuiwa. Alipata nguvu katika mshikamano na wanaharakati wenzake, kudumisha matumaini na uvumilivu kupitia changamoto kubwa.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Walt Whitman
translatedText: Walt Whitman

sourceText: Whitman experienced profound psychological distress witnessing the horrors of the Civil War as a volunteer nurse, tending to wounded soldiers. He also faced societal prejudice and personal isolation due to his openly acknowledged homosexuality.
translatedText: Whitman alipata dhiki kubwa ya kisaikolojia akishuhudia kutisha kwa Vita vya wenyewe kwa wenyewe kama muuguzi wa kujitolea, akiwahudumia askari waliojeruhiwa. Pia alikabiliwa na ubaguzi wa kijamii na kutengwa kwa kibinafsi kwa sababu ya ushoga wake uliotambuliwa wazi.

sourceText: He transmuted the trauma of war and his personal struggles into his seminal work, 'Leaves of Grass.' Through his experiences, he cultivated compassion and inclusivity, celebrating the universal value of human existence, thus overcoming his pain and achieving artistic transcendence.
translatedText: Alibadilisha kiwewe cha vita na mapambano yake ya kibinafsi katika kazi yake ya semina, 'Leaves of Grass.' Kupitia uzoefu wake, alikuza huruma na ushirikishwaji, akisherehekea thamani ya ulimwengu ya kuwepo kwa mwanadamu, hivyo kushinda maumivu yake na kufikia ustadi wa kisanii.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: He endured immense personal suffering, including the loss of two sons, the suicide of his daughter, and the destruction of his home and possessions during World War II. He also faced the moral dilemma of working under the Nazi regime and the persecution of his Jewish colleagues.
translatedText: Alivumilia mateso makubwa sana ya kibinafsi, kutia ndani kufiwa na wana wawili, kujiua kwa binti yake, na kuharibiwa kwa nyumba na mali yake wakati wa Vita vya Pili vya Ulimwengu. Pia alikabili mtanziko wa kimaadili wa kufanya kazi chini ya utawala wa Nazi na kuteswa na wenzake Wayahudi.

sourceText: Planck's recovery was marked by his unwavering dedication to science and his efforts to preserve scientific integrity amidst political turmoil. He continued his research and advocacy for academic freedom, seeking to rebuild the scientific community after the devastation of war.
translatedText: Kupona kwa Planck kuliwekwa alama kwa kujitolea kwake kwa sayansi na juhudi zake za kuhifadhi uadilifu wa kisayansi huku kukiwa na msukosuko wa kisiasa. Aliendelea na utafiti wake na utetezi wa uhuru wa kitaaluma, akitafuta kujenga upya jumuiya ya kisayansi baada ya uharibifu wa vita.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - katikati ya karne ya 20

sourceText: As a prominent pagan intellectual in an era of escalating religious and political turmoil, Hypatia faced marginalization and persecution. The growing tensions between Christianity and paganism placed her life in peril.
translatedText: Akiwa msomi mashuhuri wa kipagani katika enzi ya msukosuko wa kidini na kisiasa unaozidi kuongezeka, Hypatia alikabiliwa na kutengwa na kuteswa. Mvutano unaokua kati ya Ukristo na upagani uliweka maisha yake hatarini.

sourceText: She championed knowledge and reason, striving to maintain an intellectual community amidst conflict by teaching philosophy and science to students from diverse backgrounds. Her teachings represented the power of reason against violence and fanaticism.
translatedText: Alitetea ujuzi na akili, akijitahidi kudumisha jumuiya ya wasomi katikati ya migogoro kwa kufundisha falsafa na sayansi kwa wanafunzi kutoka asili mbalimbali. Mafundisho yake yaliwakilisha uwezo wa kufikiri dhidi ya jeuri na ushupavu wa kidini.

sourceText: Late Antiquity
translatedText: Marehemu Antiquity

sourceText: Ibn Al Haytham faced significant challenges due to the political turmoil of his era and the resistance to his novel ideas. A notable instance involved his assignment to a dam construction project by the Fatimid ruler Al-Hakim. When the project proved unfeasible according to his designs, he experienced profound disappointment and frustration.
translatedText: Ibn Al Haytham alikabiliwa na changamoto kubwa kutokana na misukosuko ya kisiasa ya zama zake na upinzani wa mawazo yake ya riwaya. Mfano mashuhuri ulihusisha mgawo wake wa ujenzi wa bwawa na mtawala wa Fatimid Al-Hakim. Mradi ulipoonekana kutotekelezeka kulingana na miundo yake, alipata kukatishwa tamaa na kufadhaika sana.

sourceText: Despite these setbacks, his passion for knowledge remained undeterred. The eventual cessation of his administrative duties freed him to focus intensely on his scholarly pursuits. During his confinement and subsequent years, he delved deeper into optics, mathematics, and scientific methodology, producing seminal works that would cement his legacy as the 'Father of Optics'.
translatedText: Licha ya shida hizi, shauku yake ya maarifa ilibaki bila kuzuiwa. Kukomeshwa kwa kazi zake za kiutawala hatimaye kulimfanya ajikite zaidi katika shughuli zake za kielimu. Wakati wa kifungo chake na miaka iliyofuata, alizama zaidi katika uchunguzi wa macho, hisabati, na mbinu ya kisayansi, akitoa kazi za mwisho ambazo zingeimarisha urithi wake kama 'Baba wa Optics'.

sourceText: Late 10th to Early 11th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 10 hadi mapema karne ya 11

sourceText: Blaise Pascal
translatedText: Blaise Pascal

sourceText: Pascal suffered from chronic illnesses and neuralgia from a young age. His life was also marked by profound religious and philosophical introspection, which caused him considerable internal turmoil. His posthumously published 'Pensées' vividly illustrates this inner conflict.
translatedText: Pascal aliugua magonjwa sugu na hijabu tangu akiwa mdogo. Maisha yake pia yaliwekwa alama ya uchunguzi wa kina wa kidini na kifalsafa, ambao ulimletea msukosuko mkubwa wa ndani. 'Pensées' yake iliyochapishwa baada ya kifo inaonyesha kwa uwazi mzozo huu wa ndani.

sourceText: Despite his physical suffering, Pascal relentlessly pursued intellectual inquiry. He transformed his suffering into an object of faith, seeking spiritual solace and answers through deep reflection on human existence's paradoxes and divine grace.
translatedText: Licha ya mateso yake ya kimwili, Pascal alifuatilia uchunguzi wa kiakili bila kuchoka. Alibadilisha mateso yake kuwa kitu cha imani, akitafuta faraja ya kiroho na majibu kupitia kutafakari kwa kina juu ya utata wa uwepo wa mwanadamu na neema ya Mungu.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17

sourceText: Mendeleev's early life was marked by poverty and the absence of his father, and his non-noble status presented obstacles to academic opportunities. Furthermore, upon the initial presentation of his periodic table, he faced skepticism and a lack of recognition from many of his peers.
translatedText: Maisha ya mapema ya Mendeleev yaliwekwa alama ya umaskini na kutokuwepo kwa baba yake, na hali yake isiyo ya heshima ilileta vizuizi kwa fursa za masomo. Zaidi ya hayo, juu ya uwasilishaji wa awali wa jedwali lake la mara kwa mara, alikabiliwa na mashaka na ukosefu wa kutambuliwa kutoka kwa wenzake wengi.

sourceText: He overcame academic adversities through relentless research and dedication. His unwavering commitment to systematically classifying elements led to the completion of the periodic table, which ultimately became recognized as his monumental achievement.
translatedText: Alishinda matatizo ya kitaaluma kupitia utafiti na kujitolea bila kuchoka. Kujitolea kwake thabiti kwa kuainisha vipengele kwa utaratibu kulisababisha kukamilika kwa jedwali la mara kwa mara, ambalo hatimaye lilitambuliwa kama mafanikio yake makubwa.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Ada Lovelace
translatedText: Ada Lovelace

sourceText: Rigorous education enforced by her mother, societal judgment due to her father's scandalous life, persistent health issues, and a struggle with gambling.
translatedText: Elimu kali iliyolazimishwa na mama yake, uamuzi wa jamii kutokana na maisha ya babake ya kashfa, masuala ya afya yanayoendelea, na mapambano dhidi ya kamari.

sourceText: She found solace and intellectual fulfillment by focusing on mathematics and logic, and by collaborating with Charles Babbage, she brought her visionary ideas to fruition.
translatedText: Alipata faraja na utimilifu wa kiakili kwa kuzingatia hisabati na mantiki, na kwa kushirikiana na Charles Babbage, alifanikisha mawazo yake ya maono.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Alan Turing
translatedText: Alan Turing

sourceText: He was prosecuted in 1952 for homosexual acts, a criminal offense at the time, and was subjected to chemical castration. This deeply impacted his career and mental well-being.
translatedText: Alifunguliwa mashitaka mwaka wa 1952 kwa vitendo vya ushoga, kosa la jinai wakati huo, na alihasiwa kwa kemikali. Hii iliathiri sana kazi yake na ustawi wa kiakili.

sourceText: Through his groundbreaking research and theoretical work, he paved the way for the digital age. His contributions were posthumously recognized, and his legacy was eventually restored and honored.
translatedText: Kupitia utafiti wake wa msingi na kazi ya kinadharia, alifungua njia kwa enzi ya dijiti. Michango yake ilitambuliwa baada ya kifo chake, na urithi wake hatimaye ulirejeshwa na kuheshimiwa.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 20

sourceText: Charles Babbage
translatedText: Charles Babbage

sourceText: The development of his groundbreaking machines, particularly the Analytical Engine, was hampered by a lack of funding and government support. He often faced financial difficulties and criticism from contemporaries who failed to grasp his vision.
translatedText: Utengenezaji wa mashine zake za kuvunja msingi, hasa Injini ya Uchambuzi, ulitatizwa na ukosefu wa fedha na usaidizi wa serikali. Mara nyingi alikabiliwa na matatizo ya kifedha na kukosolewa kutoka kwa watu wa wakati huo ambao walishindwa kufahamu maono yake.

sourceText: Despite financial constraints and technical challenges, Babbage persevered. He continuously refined his ideas, improved his designs, and tirelessly explained the significance of his work. Although his machines were not completed in his lifetime, his monumental designs profoundly influenced the development of computer science for future generations.
translatedText: Licha ya matatizo ya kifedha na changamoto za kiufundi, Babbage alivumilia. Aliendelea kuboresha mawazo yake, akaboresha miundo yake, na kueleza bila kuchoka umuhimu wa kazi yake. Ingawa mashine zake hazikukamilika katika maisha yake, miundo yake mikuu iliathiri sana maendeleo ya sayansi ya kompyuta kwa vizazi vijavyo.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: He faced political turmoil and periods of exile, which disrupted his scholarly pursuits and writing. His fame also attracted envy and opposition from some contemporaries.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na misukosuko ya kisiasa na vipindi vya uhamishoni, ambavyo vilivuruga shughuli zake za kitaaluma na uandishi. Umaarufu wake pia ulivutia wivu na upinzani kutoka kwa watu wengine wa wakati huo.

sourceText: Despite the challenges, Ibn Sina maintained an unwavering dedication to his studies. He persevered through constant research and writing, producing a vast body of work that cemented his legacy and overcame the obstacles he faced.
translatedText: Licha ya changamoto hizo, Ibn Sina alidumisha ari isiyoyumba katika masomo yake. Alivumilia kupitia utafiti na uandishi wa mara kwa mara, akitokeza kazi nyingi ambayo iliimarisha urithi wake na kushinda vizuizi alivyokabili.

sourceText: Late 10th - Early 11th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 10 - Mapema karne ya 11

sourceText: Early steam engines were inefficient and prone to frequent breakdowns, leading to business difficulties. Watt also faced significant emotional distress due to patent disputes and conflicts with business partners.
translatedText: Injini za awali za mvuke hazikuwa na ufanisi na zinakabiliwa na kuharibika mara kwa mara, na kusababisha matatizo ya biashara. Watt pia alikabiliwa na dhiki kubwa ya kihisia kutokana na mizozo ya hataza na migogoro na washirika wa biashara.

sourceText: Through persistent research and experimentation, he developed the innovative idea of a separate condenser, drastically increasing the steam engine's efficiency. Furthermore, by collaborating with Matthew Boulton, he expanded his business and resolved patent issues, overcoming his challenges.
translatedText: Kupitia utafiti unaoendelea na majaribio, alianzisha wazo bunifu la kondomu tofauti, na kuongeza kwa kiasi kikubwa ufanisi wa injini ya mvuke. Zaidi ya hayo, kwa kushirikiana na Matthew Boulton, alipanua biashara yake na kutatua masuala ya hataza, kushinda changamoto zake.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: George Stephenson
translatedText: George Stephenson

sourceText: Born into poverty with limited formal education, Stephenson faced significant disadvantages. His groundbreaking ideas were often met with skepticism and technical limitations of the era, making the path to innovation arduous.
translatedText: Alizaliwa katika umaskini na elimu ndogo rasmi, Stephenson alikabiliwa na hasara kubwa. Mawazo yake ya msingi mara nyingi yalikutana na mashaka na mapungufu ya kiufundi ya enzi hiyo, na kufanya njia ya uvumbuzi kuwa ngumu.

sourceText: He pursued self-education in mechanical engineering, working tirelessly day and night. Despite numerous setbacks and failures, Stephenson's relentless dedication to developing the steam locomotive eventually earned him recognition and paved the way for the railway age.
translatedText: Alijisomea katika uhandisi wa mitambo, akifanya kazi bila kuchoka mchana na usiku. Licha ya kushindwa na kushindwa nyingi, kujitolea kwa Stephenson bila kuchoka katika kutengeneza treni ya mvuke hatimaye kulipata kutambuliwa na kufungua njia kwa enzi ya reli.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Gregor Mendel
translatedText: Gregor Mendel

sourceText: Mendel experienced profound frustration as his groundbreaking research went unrecognized and largely forgotten during his lifetime. While his papers were published, the scientific community failed to grasp their significance immediately. He also struggled with limited time for research due to his responsibilities as a monk and educator.
translatedText: Mendel alipata mfadhaiko mkubwa kwani utafiti wake muhimu haukutambuliwa na kusahaulika kwa kiasi kikubwa enzi za uhai wake. Wakati karatasi zake zilichapishwa, jumuiya ya wanasayansi ilishindwa kufahamu umuhimu wao mara moja. Pia alihangaika na muda mfupi wa kufanya utafiti kutokana na majukumu yake kama mtawa na mwalimu.

sourceText: Despite the lack of contemporary recognition, Mendel persevered with his experiments. His work was rediscovered posthumously, leading to his acclaim as the Father of Genetics. He remained steadfast in his scientific pursuits with patience, which ultimately allowed his discoveries to be recognized.
translatedText: Licha ya ukosefu wa kutambuliwa kwa kisasa, Mendel alivumilia majaribio yake. Kazi yake iligunduliwa tena baada ya kifo, na kusababisha sifa yake kama Baba wa Jenetiki. Alibaki thabiti katika shughuli zake za kisayansi kwa subira, ambayo hatimaye iliruhusu uvumbuzi wake kutambuliwa.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Antoine Lavoisier
translatedText: Antoine Lavoisier

sourceText: As a wealthy tax collector ('fermier général') during the French Revolution, he was targeted by the revolutionary government and guillotined.
translatedText: Kama mtoza ushuru tajiri ('fermier général') wakati wa Mapinduzi ya Ufaransa, alilengwa na serikali ya mapinduzi na kupigwa risasi.

sourceText: Despite his tragic end, his scientific contributions were widely recognized posthumously, laying the foundation for modern chemistry.
translatedText: Licha ya mwisho wake wa kutisha, michango yake ya kisayansi ilitambuliwa sana baada ya kifo, na kuweka msingi wa kemia ya kisasa.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: He experienced significant frustration due to setbacks in his official career, multiple failures in civil service examinations, and the pervasive inaccuracy and confusion within existing medical texts.
translatedText: Alipata kufadhaika kwa kiasi kikubwa kutokana na vikwazo katika kazi yake rasmi, kushindwa mara nyingi katika mitihani ya utumishi wa umma, na kuenea kwa usahihi na kuchanganyikiwa ndani ya maandiko yaliyopo ya matibabu.

sourceText: Despite these adversies, driven by a steadfast desire to aid the common people, he traveled extensively for 27 years, personally collecting herbs, meticulously studying medical literature, and ultimately completing the Bencao Gangmu.
translatedText: Licha ya matatizo hayo, akichochewa na nia thabiti ya kuwasaidia watu wa kawaida, alisafiri sana kwa miaka 27, akikusanya mitishamba binafsi, akisoma kwa makini fasihi ya kitiba, na hatimaye kukamilisha Bencao Gangmu.

sourceText: Ming Dynasty
translatedText: Nasaba ya Ming

sourceText: Guglielmo Marconi
translatedText: Guglielmo Marconi

sourceText: Initially, his ideas were met with skepticism, with many dismissing them as impractical. Securing funding and convincing established scientific and industrial circles proved to be significant hurdles.
translatedText: Hapo awali, mawazo yake yalitiliwa shaka, na wengi waliyapuuza kuwa hayafai. Kupata ufadhili na kushawishi duru za kisayansi na kiviwanda zilithibitika kuwa vikwazo muhimu.

sourceText: Through persistent experimentation and demonstration, he gradually gained recognition for his technology. The successful transatlantic wireless transmission was a pivotal moment that validated his innovations.
translatedText: Kupitia majaribio na maonyesho ya kudumu, polepole alipata kutambuliwa kwa teknolojia yake. Usambazaji wa wireless wa transatlantic ulikuwa wakati muhimu ambao ulithibitisha ubunifu wake.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Robert Koch
translatedText: Robert Cook

sourceText: Initial lack of funding and skepticism from peers. He faced debate, particularly after identifying the cholera bacillus, rather than immediate acceptance.
translatedText: Ukosefu wa awali wa ufadhili na mashaka kutoka kwa wenzao. Alikabiliwa na mjadala, hasa baada ya kutambua bacillus ya kipindupindu, badala ya kukubalika mara moja.

sourceText: Through persistent experimentation and rigorous proof, he solidified his discoveries. He focused on pursuing the truth, undeterred by criticism.
translatedText: Kupitia majaribio ya kudumu na uthibitisho mkali, aliimarisha uvumbuzi wake. Alikazia kufuatia ukweli, bila kuzuiwa na ukosoaji.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: He faced familial opposition to his religious vocation, including being held captive by his own family. He also encountered internal dissent and suspicion within his Dominican order.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na upinzani wa kifamilia kwa wito wake wa kidini, ikiwa ni pamoja na kufungwa na familia yake mwenyewe. Pia alikumbana na upinzani wa ndani na tuhuma ndani ya agizo lake la Dominika.

sourceText: Aquinas persevered through sheer intellectual brilliance and steadfast dedication to his studies and faith. His prolific writings demonstrate his ability to overcome these obstacles and establish his profound legacy.
translatedText: Aquinas alistahimili kupitia ustadi kamili wa kiakili na kujitolea thabiti kwa masomo na imani yake. Maandishi yake mengi yanaonyesha uwezo wake wa kushinda vizuizi hivi na kuanzisha urithi wake wa kina.

sourceText: High Middle Ages
translatedText: Zama za Kati

sourceText: Rumi faced immense upheaval in his life, including forced migration from his homeland in Balkh (modern-day Afghanistan) due to the Mongol invasions, settling in Konya (modern-day Turkey). The most profound pain, however, was the mysterious disappearance and presumed murder of his spiritual mentor and companion, Shams-i Tabrizi, which plunged Rumi into deep grief and existential crisis.
translatedText: Rumi alikumbana na misukosuko mikubwa maishani mwake, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuhama kwa kulazimishwa kutoka nchi yake huko Balkh (Afghanistan ya kisasa) kutokana na uvamizi wa Wamongolia, wakiishi Konya (Uturuki ya kisasa). Maumivu makubwa zaidi, hata hivyo, yalikuwa ni kutoweka kwa ajabu na kudhaniwa kuwa mauaji ya mshauri na mwandamani wake wa kiroho, Shams-i Tabrizi, ambayo yalimtumbukiza Rumi katika huzuni kubwa na mgogoro uliokuwepo.

sourceText: The loss of Shams became a catalyst for Rumi's most creative and prolific period. Instead of succumbing to despair, Rumi channeled his grief and profound love for Shams into his poetry and music. He transformed his pain into ecstatic devotion, composing his monumental works, most notably the Masnavi, which became a beacon of spiritual wisdom and a testament to the transformative power of love and loss.
translatedText: Kupotea kwa Shams ikawa kichocheo cha kipindi cha ubunifu zaidi na cha mafanikio cha Rumi. Badala ya kukata tamaa, Rumi alielekeza huzuni yake na mapenzi yake makubwa kwa Shams katika ushairi na muziki wake. Aligeuza uchungu wake kuwa ibada ya kufurahisha, akitunga kazi zake kuu, haswa Masnavi, ambayo ilikuja kuwa mwanga wa hekima ya kiroho na ushuhuda wa nguvu ya mabadiliko ya upendo na hasara.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 13

sourceText: He faced intense opposition from religious authorities, accusations of heresy, and the burning of his books, leading to restrictions on his scholarly work.
translatedText: Alikabili upinzani mkali kutoka kwa mamlaka za kidini, shutuma za uzushi, na kuchomwa moto kwa vitabu vyake, jambo lililotokeza vizuizi kwa kazi yake ya kitaaluma.

sourceText: Despite persecution, his ideas were defended and his writings were secretly preserved, eventually influencing the European Renaissance and beyond.
translatedText: Licha ya mateso, mawazo yake yalitetewa na maandishi yake yalihifadhiwa kwa siri, hatimaye kuathiri Mwamko wa Ulaya na kwingineko.

sourceText: 12th Century Islamic Golden Age
translatedText: Enzi ya Dhahabu ya Kiislamu ya Karne ya 12

sourceText: William James
translatedText: William James

sourceText: Suffered from severe neurasthenia and depression in his youth, and endured difficult times with multiple career changes and personal anguish.
translatedText: Aliteswa na neurasthenia kali na unyogovu katika ujana wake, na alivumilia nyakati ngumu na mabadiliko mengi ya kazi na uchungu wa kibinafsi.

sourceText: Deeply reflected on his painful experiences, emphasizing the importance of human consciousness and will based on them, and overcame them through an active attitude towards life.
translatedText: Alitafakari kwa kina uzoefu wake wenye uchungu, akisisitiza umuhimu wa ufahamu wa binadamu na mapenzi kulingana nao, na kuwashinda kupitia mtazamo wa kazi kuelekea maisha.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Friedrich Schiller
translatedText: Friedrich Schiller

sourceText: Repression in an authoritarian environment, illness, financial hardship, and conflicts with friends.
translatedText: Ukandamizaji katika mazingira ya kimabavu, ugonjwa, ugumu wa kifedha, na migogoro na marafiki.

sourceText: He overcame adversity with an unwavering belief in freedom and human dignity, realizing his ideals through literature and establishing himself as a leading intellectual of his time.
translatedText: Alishinda dhiki kwa imani isiyoyumba katika uhuru na utu wa binadamu, akitambua maadili yake kupitia fasihi na kujiimarisha kama mwanafikra mkuu wa wakati wake.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 18 - Mapema karne ya 19

sourceText: George Washington Carver
translatedText: George Washington Carver

sourceText: Born into slavery, he faced significant health challenges as a child, including pneumonia, and the systemic racism that limited opportunities for African Americans. He was often referred to as the 'plant doctor' due to his early affinity for healing plants, but formal education was a struggle.
translatedText: Alizaliwa katika utumwa, alikabiliwa na changamoto kubwa za kiafya akiwa mtoto, ikiwa ni pamoja na nimonia, na ubaguzi wa kimfumo ambao ulipunguza fursa kwa Waamerika wa Kiafrika. Mara nyingi alijulikana kama 'daktari wa mimea' kutokana na ushirika wake wa awali wa kuponya mimea, lakini elimu rasmi ilikuwa vigumu.

sourceText: Despite these hardships, his intellectual curiosity and dedication led him to pursue higher education. He earned advanced degrees in botany and agricultural science. Carver dedicated his life to sharing his knowledge, particularly with poor Southern farmers, helping them improve soil fertility and achieve economic independence through innovative uses for common crops.
translatedText: Licha ya magumu hayo, udadisi wake wa kiakili na kujitolea kulimfanya afuate elimu ya juu. Alipata digrii za juu katika sayansi ya mimea na kilimo. Carver alijitolea maisha yake kushiriki ujuzi wake, hasa na wakulima maskini wa Kusini, kuwasaidia kuboresha rutuba ya udongo na kupata uhuru wa kiuchumi kupitia matumizi ya ubunifu kwa mazao ya kawaida.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Profound existential doubt and a spiritual crisis that led him to abandon his academic career.
translatedText: Mashaka makubwa na shida ya kiroho ambayo ilimfanya aache kazi yake ya masomo.

sourceText: Through critical examination of philosophy and a deep dive into Sufi mysticism and spiritual contemplation, he found renewed purpose and certainty.
translatedText: Kupitia uchunguzi wa kina wa falsafa na kuzama kwa kina katika mafumbo ya Kisufi na kutafakari kiroho, alipata kusudi na uhakika upya.

sourceText: Late 11th - Early 12th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 11 - Mapema karne ya 12

sourceText: Ibn Khaldun
translatedText: Ibn Khaldun

sourceText: He experienced political turmoil, imprisonment, and exile, leading to an unstable life. He also suffered the loss of family and colleagues due to the Black Death.
translatedText: Alipata misukosuko ya kisiasa, kufungwa gerezani, na uhamishoni, na kusababisha maisha yasiyokuwa na utulivu. Pia alipata hasara ya familia na wafanyakazi wenzake kutokana na Kifo Cheusi.

sourceText: Despite hardships, he never lost his passion for scholarship. Instead, he used these experiences as a foundation to write the Muqaddimah, filled with profound insights into human society and civilization.
translatedText: Licha ya magumu, hakupoteza kamwe shauku yake ya usomi. Badala yake, alitumia uzoefu huu kama msingi wa kuandika Muqaddimah, iliyojaa umaizi wa kina katika jamii ya wanadamu na ustaarabu.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 14

sourceText: In his youth, Maimonides and his family were forced to flee their home in Al-Andalus due to religious persecution under Muslim rule, living as refugees for many years. This period was marked by profound insecurity and suffering.
translatedText: Katika ujana wake, Maimonides na familia yake walilazimika kukimbia nyumba yao huko Al-Andalus kutokana na mateso ya kidini chini ya utawala wa Waislamu, wakiishi kama wakimbizi kwa miaka mingi. Kipindi hiki kiliwekwa alama ya ukosefu wa usalama na mateso makubwa.

sourceText: Despite these adversities, his pursuit of knowledge remained unyielding. Through relentless study and writing, he produced the monumental 'Mishneh Torah,' a comprehensive codification of Jewish law, and 'The Guide for the Perplexed,' a philosophical masterpiece. He transformed his personal suffering into intellectual triumph.
translatedText: Licha ya shida hizi, harakati zake za maarifa zilibaki bila kubadilika. Kupitia kusoma na kuandika bila kuchoka, alitokeza 'Mishneh Torah,' msimbo mpana wa sheria za Kiyahudi, na 'Mwongozo wa Wanaoshangaa,' kazi bora ya falsafa. Alibadilisha mateso yake binafsi kuwa ushindi wa kiakili.

sourceText: Middle Ages (12th Century)
translatedText: Zama za Kati (karne ya 12)

sourceText: Zoroaster faced significant opposition and persecution from established religious authorities and society as he attempted to spread his new faith. His teachings were often misunderstood and rejected, leading to periods of isolation and doubt.
translatedText: Zoroaster alikabiliwa na upinzani mkubwa na mnyanyaso kutoka kwa mamlaka ya kidini yenye nguvu na jamii alipojaribu kueneza imani yake mpya. Mafundisho yake mara nyingi hayakueleweka na kukataliwa, na hivyo kusababisha vipindi vya kutengwa na mashaka.

sourceText: He overcame these challenges through unwavering conviction and profound insight into truth. Through meditation and spiritual discipline, he received revelations from Ahura Mazda, solidifying his belief in the correctness of his message. He persistently preached his ideas, eventually gaining a substantial following.
translatedText: Alishinda changamoto hizi kupitia usadikisho usioyumba na ufahamu wa kina katika ukweli. Kupitia kutafakari na nidhamu ya kiroho, alipokea mafunuo kutoka kwa Ahura Mazda, yakiimarisha imani yake katika usahihi wa ujumbe wake. Aliendelea kuhubiri mawazo yake, hatimaye akapata wafuasi wengi.

sourceText: In his youth, Cervantes was captured by Barbary pirates while serving in Italy and spent five years as a slave. Upon his return to Spain, he faced financial difficulties and imprisonment, making his life far from easy.
translatedText: Katika ujana wake, Cervantes alitekwa na maharamia wa Barbary alipokuwa akihudumu nchini Italia na alitumia miaka mitano kama mtumwa. Aliporudi Uhispania, alikabili matatizo ya kifedha na kufungwa gerezani, na kufanya maisha yake kuwa rahisi.

sourceText: Despite these adversities, he never lost his passion for literature. His vivid experiences and profound understanding of human nature were transformed into immortal works like Don Quixote.
translatedText: Licha ya shida hizi, hakuwahi kupoteza shauku yake ya fasihi. Uzoefu wake wazi na uelewa wa kina wa asili ya mwanadamu ulibadilishwa kuwa kazi zisizoweza kufa kama Don Quixote.

sourceText: Spanish Golden Age
translatedText: Kihispania Golden Age

sourceText: Leo Tolstoy
translatedText: Leo Tolstoy

sourceText: Leo Tolstoy
translatedText: Leo Tolstoy

sourceText: Tolstoy grappled with profound anguish stemming from his brutal experiences in the Crimean War, conflicts with loved ones, and a lifelong spiritual crisis. The search for life's meaning and the fear of death tormented him.
translatedText: Tolstoy alikabiliana na uchungu mwingi uliotokana na matukio yake ya kikatili katika Vita vya Crimea, migogoro na wapendwa wao, na mzozo wa kiroho wa maisha yote. Utafutaji wa maana ya maisha na woga wa kifo ulimtesa.

sourceText: He sought to overcome these struggles through literary exploration and deep introspection. Tolstoy found simple truths in the lives of peasants and strived for spiritual peace through non-violent resistance and asceticism. His works are the culmination of this struggle and reflection.
translatedText: Alitafuta kushinda mapambano haya kupitia uchunguzi wa kifasihi na kujichunguza kwa kina. Tolstoy alipata ukweli rahisi katika maisha ya wakulima na alijitahidi kupata amani ya kiroho kupitia upinzani usio na jeuri na kujinyima raha. Kazi zake ni kilele cha mapambano na tafakari hii.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Murasaki Shikibu
translatedText: Murasaki Shikibu

sourceText: The early death of her mother, the limitations placed upon women pursuing scholarship, and the loneliness experienced amidst the complex human relationships of court life.
translatedText: Kifo cha mapema cha mama yake, vikwazo vilivyowekwa kwa wanawake wanaotafuta usomi, na upweke uliopatikana kati ya mahusiano magumu ya kibinadamu ya maisha ya mahakama.

sourceText: She sublimated her inner suffering by creating 'The Tale of Genji,' drawing upon her keen observation and delicate sensibility. Furthermore, through the medium of literature, she delicately depicted the lives and emotions of women of her time, thereby overcoming the limitations of her era and building her own world.
translatedText: Alipunguza mateso yake ya ndani kwa kuunda 'Hadithi ya Genji,' kwa kuzingatia uchunguzi wake wa kina na hisia dhaifu. Zaidi ya hayo, kupitia njia ya fasihi, alionyesha kwa ustadi maisha na hisia za wanawake wa wakati wake, na hivyo kushinda mapungufu ya enzi yake na kujenga ulimwengu wake mwenyewe.

sourceText: Heian Period
translatedText: Kipindi cha Heian

sourceText: Edgar Degas
translatedText: Edgar Degas

sourceText: He faced financial pressures from his father and doubts about traditional academic art training. Later in life, he suffered from progressive vision loss.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na shinikizo la kifedha kutoka kwa baba yake na mashaka juu ya mafunzo ya sanaa ya kitamaduni ya kitaaluma. Baadaye maishani, alipata shida ya kupoteza uwezo wa kuona.

sourceText: Degas pursued his unique artistic vision throughout his life, refusing to be confined by tradition and forging a new path with his distinctive perspective and techniques. Despite his failing eyesight, he explored various mediums, including sculpture, maintaining his passion for art.
translatedText: Degas alifuata maono yake ya kipekee ya kisanii katika maisha yake yote, akikataa kufungwa na mila na kuunda njia mpya na mtazamo wake tofauti na mbinu. Licha ya upungufu wa macho yake, alichunguza njia mbalimbali, ikiwa ni pamoja na uchongaji, kudumisha mapenzi yake kwa sanaa.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Oscar Wilde
translatedText: Oscar Wilde

sourceText: Persecution due to societal prejudice and moral judgment, leading to unjust imprisonment and profound suffering.
translatedText: Mateso kutokana na ubaguzi wa kijamii na hukumu ya kimaadili, na kusababisha kufungwa gerezani bila haki na mateso makubwa.

sourceText: Despite immense hardship, he maintained his passion for art, using his writing to express his truths and experiences, thereby seeking spiritual freedom.
translatedText: Licha ya magumu mengi, alidumisha shauku yake ya sanaa, akitumia maandishi yake kueleza ukweli na uzoefu wake, na hivyo kutafuta uhuru wa kiroho.

sourceText: Late 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa Karne ya 19

sourceText: Rabindranath Tagore
translatedText: Rabindranath Tagore

sourceText: He faced societal constraints and personal tragedies, yet his spirit remained unbroken, finding solace and expression through his art.
translatedText: Alikumbana na vikwazo vya kijamii na mikasa ya kibinafsi, lakini roho yake ilibaki bila kuvunjika, akipata faraja na kujieleza kupitia sanaa yake.

sourceText: Through his prolific writing and profound philosophical insights, Tagore transformed his experiences into universal messages of hope, resilience, and the interconnectedness of all beings.
translatedText: Kupitia uandishi wake mwingi na maarifa ya kina ya kifalsafa, Tagore alibadilisha uzoefu wake kuwa ujumbe wa tumaini, uthabiti, na muunganiko wa viumbe vyote.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: The pain of having her intellectual pursuits restricted due to her gender within strict religious and societal norms. She endured the threat of the Inquisition and the envy and criticism of her fellow nuns.
translatedText: Maumivu ya kuwa na shughuli zake za kiakili vikwazo kwa sababu ya jinsia yake ndani ya kanuni kali za kidini na kijamii. Alistahimili tishio la Baraza la Kuhukumu Wazushi na wivu na ukosoaji wa watawa wenzake.

sourceText: She persevered by continuing her writing and scholarly research, refusing to suppress her intellectual curiosity. She defended her convictions without yielding, even using these challenges as material for her works, deepening their literary richness.
translatedText: Alivumilia kwa kuendeleza uandishi wake na utafiti wa kitaalamu, akikataa kukandamiza udadisi wake wa kiakili. Alitetea imani yake bila kubadilika, hata akitumia changamoto hizo kama nyenzo za kazi zake, akizidisha utajiri wao wa kifasihi.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17

sourceText: Li Qingzhao
translatedText: Li Qingzhao

sourceText: She endured profound hardships, including the death of her husband, forced displacement due to the Jurchen invasion, and the loss of her cherished collection of books and art.
translatedText: Alivumilia matatizo makubwa, ikiwa ni pamoja na kifo cha mume wake, kulazimishwa kuhama kwa sababu ya uvamizi wa Jurchen, na kupoteza mkusanyiko wake wa vitabu na sanaa.

sourceText: Despite these tribulations, she continued to write poetry, meticulously collecting scattered texts and creating new works, transforming her sorrow into art.
translatedText: Licha ya dhiki hizi, aliendelea kuandika mashairi, akikusanya kwa uangalifu maandishi yaliyotawanyika na kuunda kazi mpya, akibadilisha huzuni yake kuwa sanaa.

sourceText: Song Dynasty
translatedText: Nasaba ya Wimbo

sourceText: The early death of his father and the subsequent fall of his family led to an unstable life. Even after achieving fame, he relentlessly pursued poetic perfection, grappling with deep inner turmoil.
translatedText: Kifo cha mapema cha baba yake na kuanguka kwa familia yake baadaye kulisababisha maisha yasiyokuwa na utulivu. Hata baada ya kupata umaarufu, alifuata ukamilifu wa kishairi bila kuchoka, akipambana na msukosuko mkubwa wa ndani.

sourceText: Through profound communion with nature, he grasped the essence of life. His extensive travels broadened his understanding of the world, elevating his poetic realm. His struggles were transmuted into profound verse.
translatedText: Kupitia ushirika wa kina na maumbile, alifahamu kiini cha maisha. Safari zake nyingi zilipanua uelewa wake wa ulimwengu, na kuinua ulimwengu wake wa ushairi. Mapambano yake yalibadilishwa kuwa aya ya kina.

sourceText: Edo Period (1603-1868)
translatedText: Kipindi cha Edo (1603-1868)

sourceText: William Wilberforce
translatedText: William Wilberforce

sourceText: His lifelong campaign against the slave trade was fraught with immense opposition, political pressure, and personal attacks. Health issues and bouts of despair also plagued him.
translatedText: Kampeni yake ya maisha yote dhidi ya biashara ya utumwa ilijaa upinzani mkubwa, shinikizo la kisiasa, na mashambulizi ya kibinafsi. Masuala ya afya na nyakati za kukata tamaa pia zilimsumbua.

sourceText: Fueled by deep conviction, the support of allies, and his Christian faith, he persevered through setbacks, relentlessly advocating for legislation in Parliament.
translatedText: Akiwa amechochewa na usadikisho wa kina, kuungwa mkono na washirika, na imani yake ya Kikristo, alivumilia kupitia vikwazo, akitetea bila kuchoka sheria katika Bunge.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 18 - Mapema karne ya 19

sourceText: Toussaint Louverture
translatedText: Toussaint Louverture

sourceText: He endured the brutal system of slavery and racial discrimination under French colonial rule. Furthermore, he suffered through numerous wars, internal conflicts, and betrayals during the struggle for independence.
translatedText: Alivumilia mfumo wa kikatili wa utumwa na ubaguzi wa rangi chini ya utawala wa kikoloni wa Ufaransa. Zaidi ya hayo, aliteseka kupitia vita vingi, migogoro ya ndani, na usaliti wakati wa kupigania uhuru.

sourceText: He successfully led the slave rebellion, declared Haiti's independence, abolished slavery, and worked towards rebuilding the nation. His leadership and dedication brought freedom and hope to the people of Haiti.
translatedText: Aliongoza kwa mafanikio uasi wa watumwa, akatangaza uhuru wa Haiti, akakomesha utumwa, na akafanya kazi ya kulijenga upya taifa. Uongozi wake na kujitolea kulileta uhuru na matumaini kwa watu wa Haiti.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 18 - Mapema karne ya 19

sourceText: Sojourner Truth
translatedText: Ukweli Mgeni

sourceText: Born into slavery, she endured brutal abuse, family separations, and the denial of her basic human rights. She was illiterate and faced profound racism and sexism even after gaining her freedom.
translatedText: Alizaliwa utumwani, alivumilia unyanyasaji wa kikatili, kutengana kwa familia, na kunyimwa haki zake za kimsingi za kibinadamu. Hakujua kusoma na kuandika na alikabiliwa na ubaguzi wa rangi na ubaguzi wa kijinsia hata baada ya kupata uhuru wake.

sourceText: Guided by her deep faith and an unyielding spirit, she became a powerful voice for abolition and women's rights. She used her personal experiences and eloquent oratory to awaken consciences and demand justice.
translatedText: Akiongozwa na imani yake ya kina na roho isiyobadilika, akawa sauti yenye nguvu ya kukomesha na haki za wanawake. Alitumia uzoefu wake wa kibinafsi na usemi fasaha kuamsha dhamiri na kudai haki.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19
